title: Kerala Olan

ingredients.name: ash gourd (poosanikai/white pumpkin), peeled, deseeded and cubed

subs.name: yellow pumpkin (kaddu) for a slightly sweeter olan
subs.notes: reduce cooking time by 2 minutes as pumpkin softens faster

subs.name: red cowpeas (vanpayar) for the traditional Sadya version
subs.notes: soak overnight and pressure cook 4 whistles

subs.name: fresh coconut ground with water and strained
subs.notes: use the first press as thick milk and second press as thin

subs.name: fresh coconut ground with water and strained
subs.notes: use the first press as thick milk and second press as thin

steps.text: Pressure cook the soaked black-eyed peas in enough water to cover for 3 whistles on medium flame. They should be tender but holding their shape — not mushy. Drain and set aside. If using canned peas, skip this step.

Place the cubed ash gourd and slit green chillies in a saucepan. Add the thin coconut milk and a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle boil on medium flame, then cook uncovered for 10–12 minutes until the ash gourd is completely tender and almost translucent.
Add the cooked black-eyed peas to the pot. Stir gently and simmer for 3 minutes so the peas absorb the coconut-flavoured broth.
Reduce the flame to low. Pour in the thick coconut milk and add the curry leaves. Stir gently and heat for 2 minutes — do not let the dish boil after adding the thick coconut milk or it may split.
Remove from heat. Drizzle the coconut oil over the surface without stirring — it floats as a fragrant film. Check salt and serve as part of a Sadya meal or alongside plain rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: ash-gourd → yellow pumpkin (kaddu) for a slightly sweeter olan (reduce cooking time by 2 minutes as pumpkin softens faster); lobia → red cowpeas (vanpayar) for the traditional Sadya version (soak overnight and pressure cook 4 whistles); coconut-milk → fresh coconut ground with water and strained (use the first press as thick milk and second press as thin)
